title: Elon Musk said helping create OpenAI accidentally accelerated the AI race, which ‘wasn’t really’ his intention
excerpt: Musk said that the AI race accelerated after Anthropic spun out of OpenAI, which was not his intention when he helped create the company.
content_html:
By Katherine Li

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Elon Musk says one of his biggest efforts to slow the concentration of AI power may have had the opposite effect.

Speaking to Zanny Minton Beddoes, The Economist's editor in chief, the xAI founder said he originally helped create OpenAI as "essentially a counterweight to Google" when the tech giant "had more or less a monopoly on AI," but it did not go as he had expected.

"In creating OpenAI and then having Anthropic spin out of that, and now currently Anthropics is leader in AI, I mean, these actions have actually resulted in knock-on effects that accelerated AI, which wasn't really my intention," Musk said.

"So, it just seems like all roads lead to the acceleration of AI," Musk added.

Musk has long criticized OpenAI's evolution from a nonprofit research lab into one of the world's most valuable AI companies. His feud with CEO Sam Altman has spilled into court, where Musk sued OpenAI, Altman, Microsoft, and other affiliated parties for $150 billion, alleging the company abandoned its founding mission.

In May, a federal judge in Oakland dismissed Musk's claims after a jury found they were barred by the statute of limitations. Musk has said he plans to appeal.

During the interview, Musk also revisited the origins of Anthropic.

"The reason the Anthropic team left OpenAI is because they didn't trust Sam Altman — otherwise Anthropic wouldn't exist. They would still be at OpenAI," Musk said, adding that he thinks Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is "a very principled person" who "cares about the future of the world."

Despite Musk's personal differences with Altman and other AI leaders, Musk argued that the industry's top companies should work together on safety.

"The competitors, I think, can, you know, if given sort of a week or two to review a new model, can highlight issues," said Musk. "All the competitors have an incentive to keep the others honest."

"If there's something that was worrisome and that company was not doing anything to address that risk," Musk added, "Then that would be the moment for the government to step in and take action."

Read next

Katherine Li

content_html_zh:
作者：Katherine Li 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

埃隆·马斯克表示，他为减缓人工智能力量集中度所做的最大努力之一可能会产生相反的效果。

这位 xAI 创始人在接受《经济学人》主编 Zanny Minton Beddoes 采访时表示，他最初帮助创建 OpenAI 是为了“本质上抗衡谷歌”，当时这家科技巨头“或多或少地垄断了人工智能”，但事情并没有按照他的预期发展。

“在创建 OpenAI，然后让 Anthropic 从中分离出来，现在 Anthropics 是人工智能领域的领导者，我的意思是，这些行动实际上产生了连锁反应，加速了人工智能的发展，但这并不是我真正的意图，”马斯克说。

“所以，似乎所有的道路都会导致人工智能的加速发展，”马斯克补充道。

马斯克长期以来一直批评 OpenAI 从非营利性研究实验室发展成为世界上最有价值的人工智能公司之一的过程。 他与首席执行官萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 的不和已经蔓延到法庭，马斯克起诉 OpenAI、奥尔特曼、微软和其他关联方，要求赔偿 1500 亿美元，指控该公司放弃了其创立使命。

今年五月，奥克兰的一名联邦法官驳回了马斯克的诉讼请求，因为陪审团发现马斯克的诉讼时效已被禁止。 马斯克表示他计划上诉。

在采访中，马斯克还回顾了 Anthropic 的起源。

“Anthropic 团队离开 OpenAI 的原因是他们不信任 Sam Altman——否则 Anthropic 就不会存在。 他们仍然会在 OpenAI，”马斯克说道，并补充说他认为 Anthropic 首席执行官达里奥·阿莫迪是“一个非常有原则的人”，“关心世界的未来”。

尽管马斯克与奥特曼和其他人工智能领导者存在个人分歧，但马斯克认为，行业顶尖公司应该在安全方面进行合作。

“我认为，如果给竞争对手一两周的时间来审查新模型，你知道，他们可以突出问题，”马斯克说。 “所有竞争对手都有动力让其他人保持诚实。”

马斯克补充道：“如果出现了令人担忧的事情，而该公司没有采取任何措施来应对这种风险，那么政府就应该介入并采取行动。”
